requirements.text: WHEN a fenced code block has language 'wireframe' and non-empty info string metadata, the system shall render a label div containing the escaped metadata text above the code block
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: wireframe state:surface empty renders labeled block

requirements.text: WHEN a fenced code block has language 'wireframe' and empty or absent info string, the system shall render a normal code block without a label div
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: plain wireframe renders without label

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content contains pipe-delimited table syntax, the system shall render valid HTML table elements (table, thead, tbody, tr, th, td)
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: tables render correctly

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content contains strikethrough syntax (~~text~~), the system shall render the text wrapped in strikethrough markup (del or s tags)
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: strikethrough renders correctly

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content contains a fenced code block with language 'mermaid', the system shall produce HTML output compatible with the existing processMermaidBlocks function for diagram rendering
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: mermaid blocks compatible with processMermaidBlocks

requirements.text: WHEN rendered HTML contains fenced code blocks with language class attributes, the system shall apply Prism syntax highlighting to each block based on its language
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: Prism syntax highlighting works on fenced code blocks

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content is submitted for rendering, the system shall preprocess ticket references and document references into links before markdown-to-HTML conversion
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: smart-link preprocessing continues to work

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content contains headings (h1-h6), the system shall generate id attributes using a GitHub-compatible slug algorithm matching Showdown's ghCompatibleHeaderId output format
requirements.sourceRef: assess.md: Heading ID Format Compatibility mismatch point

requirements.text: WHEN rendered HTML is sanitized, the system shall allow wireframe label markup (div elements with class attributes 'code-block-label' and 'wireframe-label') through DOMPurify
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: DOMPurify sanitization allows new label markup

requirements.text: Wireframe label text SHALL be HTML-escaped via markdown-it's built-in escapeHtml utility to prevent XSS injection
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional: label text is HTML-escaped via escapeHtml

requirements.text: Rendering performance for non-wireframe content SHALL not regress compared to the Showdown baseline
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional: no change to rendering performance

requirements.text: The showdown package SHALL be fully removed from package.json dependencies with no remaining imports in source or test files
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Non-Functional: showdown removed from direct dependencies

requirements.text: The markdown rendering pipeline order SHALL remain: preprocessMarkdown -> render -> processMermaidBlocks -> highlightCodeBlocks -> DOMPurify.sanitize
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: Pipeline preservation key pattern

requirements.text: Heading ID slug algorithm SHALL produce identical output to Showdown's ghCompatibleHeaderId for all heading text inputs
requirements.sourceRef: assess.md: Heading ID Format Compatibility mismatch point

requirements.text: IF a wireframe fence contains special HTML characters (<, >, &, ", ') in info string metadata, THEN the label div shall display the escaped characters as text content, not render them as HTML
requirements.sourceRef: Derived from C1 XSS constraint + wireframe metadata use case

requirements.text: IF a fenced code block has no language specified, THEN the system shall render a plain pre/code block without syntax highlighting or label div
requirements.sourceRef: Derived from CR Section 4: custom fence renderer only activates for wireframe language

requirements.text: IF a wireframe fence info string contains multiple metadata tokens (e.g., 'state:surface empty mode:edit'), THEN the entire info string shall appear as the label text
requirements.sourceRef: Derived from BR-1: label contains the full info string

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content is submitted for table-of-contents extraction, the system shall return heading items with correct text, level (offset by headerLevelStart), and slug ID matching the heading IDs generated by the rendering pipeline
requirements.sourceRef: assess.md: tableOfContents.ts co-migration; CR Section 5: tables/heading rendering

requirements.text: IF headerLevelStart is 3, THEN a ## heading (level 2) shall appear as level 4 in the TOC output
requirements.sourceRef: Derived from BR-11: headerLevelStart offset behavior must be preserved from Showdown migration

requirements.text: WHEN markdown content contains task list syntax (- [ ] or - [x]), the system shall render list items with disabled checkbox input elements reflecting checked state
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 5 Acceptance Criteria: task lists render correctly; Non-Ambiguity Table: must produce input with disabled/checked

requirements.text: IF a heading contains special characters (punctuation, Unicode, ampersands, e.g. 'C++ & Rust', 'Über Büros'), THEN the slug ID shall match Showdown's ghCompatibleHeaderId output character-for-character
requirements.sourceRef: Derived from C5: heading ID parity constraint; critical divergence point between slug algorithms

scenarios.title: Wireframe fence with metadata renders labeled code block
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a fenced code block with language 'wireframe' and info string 'state:surface empty'
scenarios.then: a div with class 'code-block-label wireframe-label' containing text 'state:surface empty' appears above the code block, and the code block is visible

scenarios.title: Wireframe fence without metadata renders as normal code block
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a fenced code block with language 'wireframe' and no info string metadata
scenarios.then: a standard pre/code block with class 'language-wireframe' is visible and no label div appears above it

scenarios.title: Pipe-delimited table renders as HTML table
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a pipe-delimited table with headers and two data rows
scenarios.then: a visible HTML table element contains thead, tbody, th, and td elements matching the source data

scenarios.title: Strikethrough syntax renders correctly
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing text with strikethrough syntax (~~deleted text~~)
scenarios.then: the text appears with a line through it wrapped in del or s tags

scenarios.title: Mermaid fence produces markup compatible with post-processing
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a fenced code block with language 'mermaid' and a simple graph definition
scenarios.then: the mermaid block is transformed into a mermaid container with visible diagram content

scenarios.title: Fenced code blocks receive Prism syntax highlighting
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a fenced code block with language 'typescript' and code content
scenarios.then: the code block is visible with Prism-applied syntax highlighting classes on the code content

scenarios.title: Ticket and document references are converted to links
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a bare ticket reference (MDT-001) and a document reference (README.md)
scenarios.then: the ticket reference appears as a clickable link to the ticket page and the document reference appears as a clickable link

scenarios.title: Headings generate GitHub-compatible slug IDs
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing headings at multiple levels (## Introduction, ### Sub Section)
scenarios.when: the document is rendered and the table of contents is extracted
scenarios.then: each heading has an id attribute with a GitHub-style slug (e.g., 'introduction', 'sub-section') and the TOC items reference the same slug IDs

scenarios.title: DOMPurify allows wireframe label markup through sanitization
scenarios.given: a rendered markdown document containing a wireframe label div with class 'code-block-label wireframe-label'
scenarios.then: the label div with its class attribute remains visible in the sanitized output

scenarios.title: Task list renders with disabled checkboxes
scenarios.given: a markdown document containing a task list with one unchecked item and one checked item
scenarios.then: two checkbox input elements are visible, the first unchecked and the second checked, and both are disabled

obligations.title: Replace Showdown with markdown-it as the rendering engine in useMarkdownProcessor.ts, preserving the 5-step pipeline shape
Custom markdown-it fence renderer plugin detects wireframe language + non-empty info string and emits escaped label div above code block
Remove Showdown-specific first regex (double class 'mermaid language-mermaid') from processMermaidBlocks; retain only language-mermaid pattern compatible with markdown-it output
DOMPurify config allows div with class attributes code-block-label and wireframe-label through sanitization
Add markdown-it-task-lists plugin producing input[type=checkbox] with disabled and checked attributes matching DOMPurify whitelist
Smart-link preprocessing (preprocessMarkdown) continues to run before markdown-it rendering, producing markdown string input; no behavioral change
Remove showdown from package.json dependencies; update all test files importing showdown to use markdown-it for assertion rendering; no remaining showdown imports
Create unit tests for useMarkdownProcessor output: heading IDs, code block classes, wireframe labels, Mermaid markup patterns
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Verification Gaps: unit tests for processor

obligations.title: Prism syntax highlighting (highlightCodeBlocks) continues to work with markdown-it output structure: pre > code.language-X selector patterns unchanged
Rendering performance for non-wireframe content SHALL not regress compared to Showdown baseline
Shared slugify(text) utility in src/utils/slugify.ts used by both markdown-it-anchor config and tableOfContents.ts to produce identical heading IDs matching Showdown's ghCompatibleHeaderId
obligations.sourceRef: architecture: heading ID compatibility via shared slugify

obligations.title: Refactor tableOfContents.ts to extract headings from raw markdown text via regex, strip inline markdown from captured text, and use shared slugify() for ID generation — removing Showdown dependency
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md Mismatch: tableOfContents.ts; architecture: heading extraction decoupling

obligations.title: Heading titles are wrapped in clickable anchor links with hover # and scroll offset
